name: Alex North
abstract: Alex North (born Isadore Soifer; December 4, 1910 – September 8, 1991) was an American composer best known for his many film scores, including A Streetcar Named Desire (one of the first jazz-based film scores), Viva Zapata!, Spartacus, Cleopatra, and Who's Afraid of Virginia Woolf?. He received fifteen Academy Award nominations for his w…

about_quotes.speaker: JJ Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: I’m told on pretty reliable and pretty accurate information that that’s happened to a lot of name composers. [There is] one that I can mention in particular, because everyone knows that Alex North wrote a brilliant score for Space Odyssey 2001
about_quotes.doc_title: JJ Johnson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: one of the great sensual writers was Alex North... most people didn't really realize how great Alex North was... Alex North, Alfred Newman, Max Steiner was a real hero
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
